name: Flaxseed
summary: The richest dietary source of lignans and the plant omega-3 ALA — a small seed studied mainly for cholesterol and cardiometabolic markers.
superfoodReason: By far the highest dietary source of lignans, plus soluble fiber and the plant omega-3 ALA, with meta-analysed trial evidence for improving lipids.

benefits.claim: May improve cholesterol and cardiometabolic markers
benefits.mechanism: Flaxseed combines soluble fiber (which binds bile acids so the body pulls cholesterol from the blood to replace them), lignans, and the plant omega-3 ALA. A GRADE-assessed meta-analysis of 16 randomized trials found flaxseed supplementation improved lipid profile and body weight; effects on blood pressure were smaller and less consistent.

citations.title: The effect of flaxseed supplementation on anthropometric indices, blood pressure, and lipid profile in diabetic patients: a GRADE-assessed systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (2025)

benefits.mechanism: Flaxseed is one of the densest sources of alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), the plant omega-3 the body partly converts to EPA/DHA, and its soluble fiber supports regularity and feeds gut bacteria.

citations.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: Whole flaxseeds pass through the gut largely undigested — you have to grind them to absorb the lignans and ALA. Buy them whole and grind as needed, since the ground fat goes rancid faster.

cautions.effect: Like chia, ground flax absorbs a lot of water — too little fluid alongside a big increase and it binds rather than loosens. Expect bloating and gas while your gut adjusts.
cautions.threshold: Raise the amount gradually and drink plenty with it.

citations.title: Rheological characterization of chia seed gum as a thickening agent used for dysphagia management (Foods 2024)
